title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Gestational Trophoblastic Disease: New Zealand Gynaecologic Cancer Group guidelines Publication date: 16 January 2014 This guideline outlines the management of women after the diagnosis of molar pregnancy in NZ as agreed to by the NZ Gynaecological Cancer Group. Publication Cancer

## Gestational Trophoblastic Disease: New Zealand Gynaecologic Cancer Group guidelines
This guideline outlines the management of women after the diagnosis of molar pregnancy in NZ as agreed to by the NZ Gynaecological Cancer Group.
- Psychoactive Substances: Code of Manufacturing Practice Publication date: 16 January 2014 The Psychoactive Substances Code of Manufacturing Practice and the accompanying Implementation Plan detail the quality control requirements for manufacturers of psychoactive substances and products Publication Psychoactives

## Psychoactive Substances: Code of Manufacturing Practice
The Psychoactive Substances Code of Manufacturing Practice and the accompanying Implementation Plan detail the quality control requirements for manufacturers of psychoactive substances and products
- National Health Emergency Plan: National Reserve Supplies Management and Usage Policies, 3rd edition Publication date: 5 December 2013 This document describes policy for the management and use of national reserve supplies, and details the principal responsibilities of the Ministry and district health boards in managing and using these significant national resources. Publication Emergency management

## National Health Emergency Plan: National Reserve Supplies Management and Usage Policies, 3rd edition
This document describes policy for the management and use of national reserve supplies, and details the principal responsibilities of the Ministry and district health boards in managing and using these significant national resources.
- Emergency management
- Measures for the Prevention and Control of Clostridium difficile Infection Publication date: 26 November 2013 This publication is a practical tool to assist care providers to follow best practice, evidence based processes for prevention, surveillance, and diagnosis of Clostridium difficile infection in the health care setting. Publication Nursing

## Measures for the Prevention and Control of Clostridium difficile Infection
This publication is a practical tool to assist care providers to follow best practice, evidence based processes for prevention, surveillance, and diagnosis of Clostridium difficile infection in the health care setting.
- New Zealand Framework for Dementia Care Publication date: 21 November 2013 This publication provides a guide that district health boards (DHBs) can use as they work with primary, secondary and community health and social support services to develop clear, consistent, well-resourced and easily accessible dementia care pathways. The aim of the publication is to help people with dementia and their families and whānau to maximize their independence and wellbeing by reducing stigmas and providing clear, comprehensive information and an integrated, holistic approach to dementia care and support. Publication Older people (65+)

## New Zealand Framework for Dementia Care
This publication provides a guide that district health boards (DHBs) can use as they work with primary, secondary and community health and social support services to develop clear, consistent, well-resourced and easily accessible dementia care pathways. The aim of the publication is to help people with dementia and their families and whānau to maximize their independence and wellbeing by reducing stigmas and providing clear, comprehensive information and an integrated, holistic approach to dementia care and support.
- Health Literacy and the Prevention and Management of Skin Infections Publication date: 13 November 2013 The report identifies effective health literacy interventions to strengthen the prevention and management of skin infections for Māori children under 15 years of age and includes a set of skin infection resources for Māori parents/caregivers. Publication Diseases & conditions Health literacy Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Māori health

## Health Literacy and the Prevention and Management of Skin Infections
The report identifies effective health literacy interventions to strengthen the prevention and management of skin infections for Māori children under 15 years of age and includes a set of skin infection resources for Māori parents/caregivers.
- Diseases & conditions
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
- Medicines Management Guide for Community Residential and Facility-based Services – Disability, Mental Health and Addiction Publication date: 15 October 2013 This Medicines Management Guide is a reference tool for managers of community residential and facility-based respite services in the disability, mental health and addiction sectors Publication Disability Mental health

## Medicines Management Guide for Community Residential and Facility-based Services – Disability, Mental Health and Addiction
This Medicines Management Guide is a reference tool for managers of community residential and facility-based respite services in the disability, mental health and addiction sectors
- Guidelines for the Management of Lead-based Paint Publication date: 1 September 2013 Information on handling lead-based paint and avoiding lead poisoning for painting contractors, property owners/managers, safety and health practitioners. Publication Environmental health

## Guidelines for the Management of Lead-based Paint
Information on handling lead-based paint and avoiding lead poisoning for painting contractors, property owners/managers, safety and health practitioners.
- Environmental health
- Primary Care for Pacific People: A Pacific and Health Systems Approach Publication date: 13 March 2013 This report aims to improve knowledge about the most effective ways to improve both Pacific peoples' access to and use of primary care.  The research highlights how Pacific could accesses and utilise primary health care by addressing the issues around transport, cost and language. Publication Pacific health Primary health care

## Primary Care for Pacific People: A Pacific and Health Systems Approach
This report aims to improve knowledge about the most effective ways to improve both Pacific peoples' access to and use of primary care.  The research highlights how Pacific could accesses and utilise primary health care by addressing the issues around transport, cost and language.
- Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Older People: A background paper Publication date: 31 January 2013 Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Older People: A background paper assists health practitioners to provide advice that enables the majority of older people in New Zealand to lead longer, healthier and more independent lives. Publication Alcohol Long-term conditions Older people (65+) Asian health Māori health Pacific health Physical activity

## Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Older People: A background paper
Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Older People: A background paper assists health practitioners to provide advice that enables the majority of older people in New Zealand to lead longer, healthier and more independent lives.
- Long-term conditions
- Guidelines on Physical Activity for Older People (aged 65 years and over) Publication date: 30 January 2013 The Guidelines on Physical Activity for Older People (aged 65 years and over) provide information for health practitioners, physical activity professionals and community fitness providers Publication Older people (65+) Physical activity

## Guidelines on Physical Activity for Older People (aged 65 years and over)
The Guidelines on Physical Activity for Older People (aged 65 years and over) provide information for health practitioners, physical activity professionals and community fitness providers
- 2008/09 New Zealand Adult Nutrition Survey data tables Publication date: 12 December 2012 Selected findings presented in A Focus on Nutrition: Key findings from the 2008/09 NZ Adult Nutrition Survey and some additional results are available in Excel format. Publication Adults (19–64 years) Older people (65+) Surveys Nutrition

## 2008/09 New Zealand Adult Nutrition Survey data tables
Selected findings presented in A Focus on Nutrition: Key findings from the 2008/09 NZ Adult Nutrition Survey and some additional results are available in Excel format.
- Adults (19–64 years)
- Rauemi Atawhai – A guide to developing health education resources in New Zealand Publication date: 5 June 2012 Rauemi Atawhai has been developed to help the Ministry of Health and its contractors produce effective and appropriate health education resources that meet the needs of the intended audience, are easy to understand, and support improved health literacy. Publication Health literacy Health system Primary health care

## Rauemi Atawhai – A guide to developing health education resources in New Zealand
Rauemi Atawhai has been developed to help the Ministry of Health and its contractors produce effective and appropriate health education resources that meet the needs of the intended audience, are easy to understand, and support improved health literacy.
- Guidelines for Implementing the Prohibition on the Display of Tobacco Products Publication date: 17 April 2012 These guidelines provide information to sellers of tobacco products to ensure that they understand the new obligations placed on them when the prohibition on tobacco displays comes into force on 23 July 2012. Publication Tobacco control

## Guidelines for Implementing the Prohibition on the Display of Tobacco Products
These guidelines provide information to sellers of tobacco products to ensure that they understand the new obligations placed on them when the prohibition on tobacco displays comes into force on 23 July 2012.
- Consensus Statement on Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in New Zealand Publication date: 14 March 2012 The Ministry of Health and Cancer Society of New Zealand, with support from ACC, have developed a Consensus Statement on Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in New Zealand. Publication Adults (19–64 years) Child health Older people (65+) Youth health Vitamin D

## Consensus Statement on Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in New Zealand
The Ministry of Health and Cancer Society of New Zealand, with support from ACC, have developed a Consensus Statement on Vitamin D and Sun Exposure in New Zealand.
